title: I’m looking for a No-Code platform to create trading bots.
author: NaveGadget
selftext:
Hello everyone,   
How are you doing?   
  
I’ve been trading manually for several years with disastrous results, mainly because of emotional bias, which led me to break my strategy out of fear or greed. That’s why I’ve started looking for platforms to create bots without knowing how to program, since I don’t want to waste more time or money trading manually.   
  
I have no knowledge of programming and no budget to hire a programmer to manage it for me. I’ve already tried generic AIs like ChatGPT, which only made me waste time, as they just throw compilation errors, and in the end I don’t even know what kind of bot they built after so many code modifications. Does anyone here have knowledge of or has used any No-Code platform to automate strategies?   
  
Thanks in advance for your replies.

comments.author: TheKillerScope
comments.body: What type of bots? For crypto?

replies.author: NaveGadget
replies.body:
Good afternoon.   
Mainly for currencies at first, but I’m open to trying it with different assets.

Thank's!

comments.author: oeritas
comments.body: I think you should try https://aistudio.google.com/. It seems much simpler than learning Claude code to develop locally or laboriously testing code one by one with ChatGPT (not Codex). There might be some limitations since there's no backend, but from what I've tested, (speaking in crypto) WebSocket connections work very well, and if you refer to the docs for CCXT or APIs, you can resolve results or errors smoothly—it should work out fine. For stocks, using Polygon or other data feeds, creating multiple rule-based strategies within the frontend would be quick. If it works well, let me know!

comments.author: regnull
comments.body: I don’t know if this fits your use case, but I’m working on an AI investing platform that generates trade suggestions. You can create a bot portfolio, the suggestions are auto-executed (paper trading). You “program” it by writing your portfolio goal, and the AI monitors how well your portfolio is aligned with the goal and creates trades as needed. There is no auto trading with real money, but it’s coming later. https://portfoliogenius.ai

comments.author: AtomikTrading
comments.body: We are actually building this I’d love for you to be a tester once ready

comments.author: NaveGadget
comments.body:
Thank you very much for the input, truly.   
I’ll take a look at everything you’ve mentioned. On my side, I’ll keep searching as well, and anything I come across I’ll share with you to hear your thoughts.   
  
I really value your feedback.

comments.author: Patient-Bumblebee
comments.body:
Everstrike does this.

You can describe your strategy in words and an LLM will trade it. I haven't coded bots or spun up servers since 2 years ago.

replies.author: NaveGadget
replies.body: Thank you very much for the information

comments.author: Puzzleheaded_Ad_4478
comments.body:
I think [3commas.io](https://app.3commas.io/auth/registration?utm_source=referral&utm_medium=cabinet&c=tc2096560)  can help you. You describe your trading strategy to their chatbot and it helps you build your bot. 

No need to have programming skills.

comments.author: ok-hacker
comments.body:
Totally feel you on the emotional bias thing – that's what pushed me from manual trading into building AI-driven agents in the first place.



If you're coming from zero-code, the two things that really matter are:

\- How easy it is to express your strategy in plain language (not wiring 50 blocks together)

\- How much of the \*end-to-end\* flow it handles: signal generation, risk management, and actual execution, not just "if RSI < 30 then buy".



The pattern that works best for folks like you is thinking in terms of \*\*agents\*\* instead of classic bots. Bots usually just execute hard-coded rules; agents maintain a portfolio objective, monitor the market continuously, and adjust positions toward that goal while explaining why they did what they did (e.g. "reduced BTC exposure because volatility spiked and your max drawdown rule kicked in"). That transparency is huge when your main enemy is fear/greed, because you can see the reasoning instead of guessing whether the system "broke".



As a builder, I've leaned into Solana for this because sub-second, low-fee execution lets an agent rebalance or hedge frequently without fees eating you alive, which is critical if you're trying to systematize your emotional discipline rather than fire off a few slow, manual trades. The other big unlock is democratizing what used to be "portfolio manager" behavior (something people used to pay 2% AUM for) so you can describe your risk tolerance and goals in plain language and let the agent handle both decision-making and execution for you.



If you're open to trying something that's exactly in this direction, I'm building an autonomous trading agent platform that lets you connect accounts and have an AI agent manage a crypto/stock portfolio while explaining each move in human language – you can check it out here: https://app.andmilo.com/?code=@karsus.

comments.author: gumifan1991
comments.body: bro use Cod3x. It's so fun. Kind of like a research tool but if you pair it with Claude/ChatGPT you're basically unstoppable.

replies.author: NaveGadget
replies.body:
Hey! So, yes, I found three sites. I’ve been researching them and have already decided on one. Here’s my reasoning:

I looked at **FxDreema**, but I ruled it out. It looks a bit clunky, and honestly, the website didn't inspire much confidence. Plus, it asks for some indicators that aren't on the platforms, and it doesn't seem intuitive to use at all.

Then there’s **StrategyQuant**. It’s very expensive, and you need some actual knowledge to use the tool. I also did some digging and found out you need a 'somewhat powerful' computer to handle the bot creation processes and optimizations, haha.

The last one I found is **Techain.ai**. It looks pretty intuitive and has several options for creating bots. What I like most is that it creates the bot in either MQL4 or MQL5—you get to choose the format. Plus, it’s the cheapest of the three.

I’ll sign up in the next few days and keep you updated. One question though: is it allowed to post screenshots of a bot I create on the platform? I'd like to show you guys how it works, but I don't want to break any of this sub's rules.

Anyway, I’ll let you know how it goes with the platform. Happy holidays, everyone!
